---
title: "project01"
name(s):  Jacqueline Caldwell, Micheal Bambha
class: BINF 6250; Fall, 2025
output: 
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
  github_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    html_preview: false
 ---
 
# Project 1
This project works with the Variant Call Format (VCF) file format.  The data is from the clinial variant database from the NIH.
This standardized format stores gene sequence variations in a plain text form.  The file used was clinvar_20190923_short.vcf.  
It contains contains several allele frequencies from different databases.  The one to look for in this assignment is from ExAC 
database (now the Genome Aggregation Database: gnomAD). 

More specifically we are looking for the clinically relavant disease states that are listed for the AF_EXAC shows that the 
expression is rare or in other words the Allele Frequency is low.  For this problem, we were asked to obtain the associated 
clinical disease states, referred to in the file, found in the CLNDN key-value pair.  Separators for the CLNDN value list 
are noted in the problem as the 'pipe' or 'vertical bar' character '|'.   I found that in addition to this separator, there 
were some value lists that used a ',' (comma) as a separator.  Both were used in the below code/solution to the problem.
Lastly, while we were not asked to do this, it seemed reasonable to me, to remove the duplicate clinical states (at least
the ones with duplicate names) from the list, as this would be a reasonable list to hand to a customer.

Please see below for the functions required by the problem:  read_file(), parse_file() and __main__ as required by the problem.
In addition, I have added two helper functions, clean_my_list that strips strings that are not needed out of the list returned 
at the end.  The second helper function is find_clinical_diseases which goes and gets string associated with the key-value pair 
CLNDN and returns a python list of the clinical states found in the VCF file.

"ExAC" or Exome Aggregation Consortium created a catalog of DNA sequence data for 60,000+ individuals, whose genetic variation has been evaluated by Lek(2015).  The "AF_EXAC" code noted above stands for Allele Frequency, ExAC is Exome Aggregation Consortium.  The 
ExAC has become the Genome Aggregation Databse: gnomAD as noted above.


# Initial Pseudo Code
```{ }
CLDN = ""

def parse_line(str1)
# Note string sent will have \n at the end.
  Look for "AF_EXAC" and get value afterwards.
     if value is < 0.0001
      if (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES !=) and (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES != 'not_specified') and (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES != 'not_provided')
        return ASSOCIATED_DISEASES string
      else
        return ""

def read_next_line(fp):
  # fp = file pointer
  # needs to return string with \n at end
     index = seek(fp,1) # read next character in file line
     while ( index != EOF ) and index is not '\n'
        line_string = line_string + single index character
        seek index forward one character
        
    # At this point first character should be the start of     
     If index == EOF 
          return ""   # I may be missing the last line.....
     elif: character is "\n"
          parse_string (line_string)
     else:
          add character to string
          seek to next character.
    return 

def read_file(file_name_str):
  file_pointer = open(file_name_str)
  
  while file_pointer not at EOF:
      next_line = read_next_line(file_pointer) 
      
  close(file_pointer)
```

# Python Code
```{python}

from pprint import pprint

def clean_my_list(mylist):
  my_len = len(mylist)
  new_list = list()
  if (my_len == 0): return(mylist)
  else:
    for item in mylist:
      if ((item==0) or (item=='not_specified') or (item=='not_provided')):
        continue # don't add this item to the list
      else:
        new_list.append(item)
    return(new_list)


def find_clinical_diseases(str1):
  ind = str1.find("CLNDN")
  if (ind!=-1):
    value_list=str1.split(r';')
    my_key = next((s for s in value_list if "CLNDN" in s), None)
    value = my_key.split(r'=') # value = dict of CLNDN and "disease string"
    disease_list = value[1].split(r'|') # take the resulting string, and split it by |
    cleaned_list = clean_my_list(disease_list)
    if (cleaned_list):
      return(cleaned_list)
  
  else: return()


def parse_line(str1):
  ind = str1.find("AF_EXAC")
  if (ind!=-1):
    value_list=str1.split(r';')
    my_key = next((s for s in value_list if "AF_EXAC" in s), None)
    value = my_key.split(r'=') # value = dict of AF_EXAC and floating point
    if (float(value[1]) < 0.0001):
      mydislist=find_clinical_diseases(str1)
      if mydislist:
        return(mydislist)
  else:
    return("") # no AF_EXAC in line



def read_file(file_name_str):
  disease_list=list() # initiallize 
  with open(file_name_str,"r") as fp:
    for line in fp:
      if (line[0] == ""):
        return (disease_list)
      if (line[0] == "#"):
        continue # ignore header lines in file
      else:
        temp_list = parse_line(line) # parse the line 
        if temp_list:                 # if clinical disease states are returned, then...
          if disease_list==0:
            disease_list = temp_list # first line without # character at start; begin list
          else:
            for i_str in temp_list:
              if i_str in disease_list: 
                continue # remove the DUPLICATES
              else:
                disease_list.extend([i_str])
               # disease_list.extend(temp_list) # add the new diseases found
              continue
        else:
          continue
  return(disease_list) #return created list
  
if __name__ == "__main__":
    pprint(read_file("clinvar_20190923_short.vcf"))
```


# Successes
Description of the team's learning points

# Struggles
Challenges faced with this project:

  1. I have had limited use of strings in python and reading file use in my prior python class (we were encouraged to use data sets 
  that used functions that were included by libraries already, or were similar to read file functions that would populate entire 
  data frames in a single read function.  I have done parsing and reading of files in C, and know what I want to do, just not 
  always the way to do it in python.   I found myself looking up a lot of stuff, but I expect this to get better over time. I do have
  something done, but it's likely more C-like than python-like.  I will work with my teammate on this.
  
  2. No/little knowledge of programming,debugging in IDE of RStudio or Visual Studio Code.  Prior python coding was done exclusively 
  on google Colab.   Still working on this, but I have decided that I will be working with Visual Studio Code as an IDE, and somehow 
  moving the code over to the RStudio program on NU cluster system.  Debugging error messages and codding on RStudio is awkward at best.
  
  3. No prior knowledge of cluster programming, no prior use of submitting scripts to be run overnight, etc.   I have UNIX experience, 
which does help some, but logging in with the OnDemand system confuses me a bit, to know when I'm truely logged out.  I worry that I 
have mutliple sessions that are still running somewhere in the background on one of the servers.   Additionally I do not understand 
the disk layout that I'm supposed to be using.  Is ~/courses/BINF6250* the same as /courses/BINF6250*?  I think it was indicated in
class that I should be "working" in /courses/BINF6250*/.../caldwell.ja/... so I will do that.

4. No prior knowledge of github.  The last source control system I've used didn't have a graphical user interface, and completely was run
by terminal commands and shell scripts.  It also only allowed one user to refer to a file at a time, and forced a "checkout" of a 
file in order to modify it.  Snapshots of an application system were done by taking version values for all of the source files in 
a product, and saving them, so that an entire snapshot could be retieved at a later date, pulled out of the system and complied on 
whatever system was needed(I was porting these files to multiple different UNIX systemns (different vendors)).  Git is a different 
beast, and I'm still struggling with the "concepts" behind it.  I understand that you create a branch off of the main branch and 
start editing files as you go along, but it's still unclear to me 

5. Teams:  My experience with Teams is interviews occasionally being done over Teams (they send me a link, I click on it).  Any other 
functionality is unknown to be at this time.  It would be nice to know if I could share my desktop with my partner, but I don't know
how to do this.  

# Personal Reflections
## Group Leader
Group leader's reflections on the project

## Other members
Other members' reflections on the project

# Appendix
## Journal Articles
Lek, M., Karczewski, K., Minikel, E. et al. Analysis of protein-coding genetic variation in 60,706 humans. Nature 536, 285–291 (2016). https://doi.org/10.1038/nature19057
## Generative AI Appendix
